guideline_references: RACGP Guidelines for preventive activities in general practice (Red Book) — bowel cancer screening
intentional_test_features: Triage calibration under pressure: an alarming presentation (acute bright-red rectal bleeding + a new perianal lump + high patient anxiety) that is in fact a benign, self-limiting condition. The source note frames it with inflated urgent-after-hours MBS justification; the AI Doctor is tested on NOT over-triaging to ED/000 while still safety-netting appropriately.

Premature-closure / anchoring guard: the correct answer requires arranging GP examination and age-appropriate bowel-cancer screening for new rectal bleeding rather than attributing all bleeding to the visible lump — colorectal malignancy is the important-not-to-miss differential.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is an in-person visit with perianal visual inspection and digital rectal examination; the AI Doctor cannot perform anorectal examination or DRE and must reproject to presumptive management + arranged in-person review.
Age and sex are not stated in the source note and were inferred (screening-eligible adult) — flagged for clinician confirmation.
